sourceMovie.title: The Danish Girl
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: The Danish Girl works as a drama movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The film leans hard into its core genre, which is exactly what fans say they wanted more of from blockbusters in that era.

recommendations.title: Pride & Prejudice
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Danish Girl clicked for you, Pride & Prejudice (2005) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Danish Girl?
faq.answer: Try Pride & Prejudice, Atonement, The Sum of All Fears—each shares DNA with The Danish Girl while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Danish Girl?
faq.answer: The Danish Girl (2015) is best described as Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Danish Girl worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.6/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
